prompt:
Take the provided reference image and reproduce it PIXEL-FAITHFULLY — same two-hand composition (one hand cradling the capped horizontally-held vial, other hand holding a pen mid-stroke on the white label, blood pooled along the lower side of the horizontal vial), same line-art style on pure white background, same line weight, same cap with knurling, same horizontal vial orientation, same DOB date '07/14/89' on the label, same patient-information-leaflet aesthetic. DO NOT change the hands, the composition, the framing, the line weight, the cap, the pen, the date numerals, or the style.

THE ONLY MODIFICATION — vial DRAMATICALLY SMALLER: the vial is currently too big. Shrink the entire vial UNIFORMLY to roughly the SIZE OF AN ADULT PINKY FINGER — the vial's diameter should be only slightly thinner than the supporting fingertip holding it, and its length only about as long as a pinky finger. Picture: a tiny finger-thin glass tube about pinky-sized. Both the diameter AND the length shrink together by ~50% so the slim aspect ratio is preserved (length ≈ diameter for the short stubby horizontal pose). The cap, label, and blood scale down proportionally with the vial. The hands stay the SAME SIZE — only the vial (and its attached elements) shrink, so the hands look proportionally MUCH larger and the vial reads as a tiny pinky-sized object pinched between them.

Do not modify anything else — no changes to the hand poses, the pen, the writing action, the date numerals (still '07/14/89'), the cap design, the line weight, the background, or the style. Pure black line-art on white with the red blood fill as the only color. No frame, no border around the canvas, no other labels.
